notes.note: A contio is an informal public meeting called by a magistrate (commonly a tribune of the plebs) at which speakers harangued the people without the assembly's voting; Clodius's chief instrument throughout 58--56 BC.

Commutata persona: a deliberate echo of Cicero's earlier letter to Nepos (Fam. 5.4) in which he proposed that Nepos take Quintus Cicero's place in his affection, the Caecilii Metelli having become Cicero's brother-figures by reconciliation.
Probably M. Lollius Palicanus or a freedman of his; here clearly Nepos's man of business at Rome, charged with the books of the Spanish province.
